recommendations.rationale: Immediate first-line investigation. Rules out haemorrhagic stroke before thrombolysis. Detects ICH within minutes of onset.
recommendations.india_context: Mandatory before IV tPA administration. Available at all stroke-ready centres. FAST protocol — door to CT within 25 minutes.
recommendations.special_notes: Cannot reliably detect acute ischaemic stroke in first 6 hours. MRI diffusion is more sensitive.

recommendations.rationale: Most sensitive for acute ischaemic stroke — detects within 30 minutes of onset. DWI shows restricted diffusion. Essential for wake-up stroke and late presentation.
recommendations.india_context: Preferred when CT is inconclusive and time permits. Essential for posterior fossa strokes missed on CT.
recommendations.special_notes: DWI-FLAIR mismatch guides thrombolysis in unknown onset strokes.

recommendations.rationale: Identifies large vessel occlusion (LVO) for mechanical thrombectomy decision. Detects dissection, aneurysm, severe stenosis.
recommendations.india_context: Perform alongside NCCT in thrombectomy-capable centres. LVO present in 25–30% of anterior circulation strokes.
recommendations.special_notes: ASPECTS score on NCCT and CTA together guide thrombectomy eligibility.

recommendations.rationale: Identifies ischaemic penumbra vs. core infarct. Guides thrombectomy in extended time window (6–24 hours, DAWN/DEFUSE criteria).
recommendations.india_context: Available at advanced stroke centres. Use in late-presenting patients (>6h) for thrombectomy selection.
recommendations.special_notes: Requires RAPID or similar software for automated analysis.

recommendations.rationale: Non-contrast alternative to CTA for vessel assessment — dissection, stenosis, aneurysm.
recommendations.india_context: Use when CT contrast is contraindicated. Time-of-flight MRA available at most 1.5T MRI centres.

recommendations.rationale: Immediate first-line for thunderclap headache to detect SAH. Sensitivity >90% in first 6 hours if read by experienced radiologist.
recommendations.india_context: Mandatory and urgent in all thunderclap headaches. Sensitivity drops to <50% after 24–48 hours.
recommendations.special_notes: If CT negative but SAH clinically suspected — proceed to LP for xanthochromia (>12h after headache onset).

recommendations.rationale: Identifies aneurysm as source of SAH. Detects aneurysms >3 mm with high accuracy.
recommendations.india_context: Perform immediately after confirming SAH on NCCT. Guides neurosurgical or neurointerventional planning.
recommendations.special_notes: DSA (conventional angiography) is gold standard if CTA negative with confirmed SAH.

recommendations.rationale: More sensitive than CT for subacute SAH (>6h). FLAIR detects subarachnoid blood. T2* GRE/SWI detects haemorrhage.
recommendations.india_context: Use when CT inconclusive and LP not available or contraindicated.
recommendations.special_notes: FLAIR positive for SAH may persist for weeks — aids diagnosis in delayed presentations.

recommendations.rationale: Investigation of choice. Detects structural causes — tumour, hippocampal sclerosis, cortical dysplasia, cavernoma. Should include epilepsy protocol (FLAIR, T1, T2, DWI, T2*).
recommendations.india_context: All new onset seizures in adults require MRI. Epilepsy protocol sequences are essential.
recommendations.special_notes: Standard T1/T2 may miss subtle lesions. Request epilepsy protocol: FLAIR, coronal hippocampal cuts, T2*, DWI.

recommendations.rationale: Immediate investigation when MRI not available or patient unstable. Excludes haemorrhage, large mass, hydrocephalus.
recommendations.india_context: Perform urgently in first seizure. Proceed to MRI after initial CT if no acute finding.
recommendations.special_notes: CT misses most epileptogenic lesions seen on MRI. Always plan MRI follow-up.

recommendations.rationale: For suspected mass lesion with enhancement or CNS infection.
recommendations.india_context: Consider when neurocysticercosis (common in India) or brain abscess is suspected.
recommendations.special_notes: Neurocysticercosis is the most common cause of new onset seizures in adults in India. Ring-enhancing lesions.

recommendations.rationale: Recommended based on clinical decision rules (NICE, PECARN, Canadian CT Head Rule). Identifies intracranial haemorrhage, skull fracture.
recommendations.india_context: Perform if any high-risk criteria: GCS <15, suspected open/depressed skull fracture, seizure, vomiting >1 time, age >65, anticoagulants, dangerous mechanism.
recommendations.special_notes: Canadian CT Head Rule: apply to GCS 13–15 with LOC/amnesia. PECARN for paediatric patients.

recommendations.rationale: Does not detect intracranial injury. A normal skull X-ray does not exclude significant head injury.
recommendations.india_context: Skull X-ray has been superseded by CT. Do not use for acute head injury assessment.
recommendations.special_notes: Only acceptable if CT is unavailable — refer patient to CT-capable facility.

recommendations.rationale: More sensitive than CT for DAI (diffuse axonal injury), brainstem injury, and subtle haemorrhage. Not practical in acute setting.
recommendations.india_context: Use for post-acute assessment when neurological symptoms persist despite normal CT.

recommendations.rationale: Investigation of choice. Characterises tumour — location, size, oedema, enhancement pattern, multicentricity, leptomeningeal spread.
recommendations.india_context: All suspected brain tumours require MRI with gadolinium contrast. Include T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, T1+contrast, MRS if available.
recommendations.special_notes: Advanced sequences: MR Spectroscopy for grading, perfusion for neovascularisation, functional MRI for eloquent cortex mapping pre-operatively.
contraindicated_if: severe renal failure (gadolinium — risk of NSF)

recommendations.rationale: Alternative when MRI unavailable or contraindicated. Identifies most tumours, haemorrhage, hydrocephalus, and midline shift.
recommendations.india_context: Interim investigation while arranging MRI. Proceed to MRI for complete characterisation.

recommendations.rationale: For differentiating tumour recurrence from radiation necrosis. Also for detecting systemic primary in brain metastasis workup.
recommendations.india_context: Use selectively. High cost. Primarily for post-treatment monitoring.
recommendations.special_notes: FDG-PET limited for brain (high background activity). Amino acid PET (FDOPA, MET) superior for gliomas — not widely available in India.

recommendations.rationale: First-line and most informative. Identifies hippocampal and medial temporal lobe atrophy (Alzheimer's), white matter ischaemia (vascular dementia), frontal/temporal atrophy (FTD), and reversible causes (tumour, SDH, NPH).
recommendations.india_context: Alzheimer's disease and vascular dementia are most common in India. Reversible causes (vitamin B12 deficiency, hypothyroidism — metabolic) must be excluded before imaging. MRI brain available at most district hospitals.
recommendations.special_notes: Medial temporal lobe atrophy (MTA) scale for Alzheimer's. White matter hyperintensities (Fazekas scale) for vascular dementia. Look for NPH triad (enlarged ventricles, tight sulci at vertex, wide Sylvian fissures).

recommendations.rationale: Acceptable alternative when MRI unavailable. Rules out structural causes (tumour, SDH, NPH, large infarcts) but less sensitive for hippocampal atrophy and white matter changes.
recommendations.india_context: Use when MRI is unavailable or contraindicated. Still identifies most reversible structural causes of cognitive decline.
recommendations.special_notes: NPH: Evans index >0.3 (ventricular size relative to brain width). Can identify large vessel disease and calcifications.

recommendations.rationale: Shows characteristic hypometabolism patterns: parieto-temporal (Alzheimer's), frontotemporal (FTD), asymmetric frontoparietal (CBS). Differentiates dementia subtypes when structural MRI is equivocal.
recommendations.india_context: Available at AIIMS and major PET centres. Use when dementia type is unclear or atypical features present.
recommendations.special_notes: Alzheimer's: posterior cortical, precuneus, cingulate hypometabolism. FTD: frontal/anterior temporal hypometabolism.

recommendations.rationale: Directly detects amyloid plaques (Alzheimer's pathology). High negative predictive value — negative scan excludes Alzheimer's. Used in atypical or early-onset dementia.
recommendations.india_context: Very limited availability in India. Reserve for selected cases where AD diagnosis is uncertain and changes management.
recommendations.special_notes: A positive amyloid PET does not confirm AD (amyloid alone without tau is not sufficient). Combine with clinical assessment.

scenarios.title: Spinal Cord Compression / Myelopathy
references: NICE Metastatic Spinal Cord Compression Guidelines

recommendations.modality: MRI Whole Spine (with contrast if malignancy suspected)
recommendations.rationale: Gold standard and mandatory in all suspected myelopathy. Identifies disc prolapse, ligamentum flavum hypertrophy, epidural metastasis, abscess, haematoma, and intrinsic cord pathology. Whole spine MRI is essential — multilevel disease is common.
recommendations.india_context: Cervical spondylotic myelopathy is the most common cause in India. Spinal tuberculosis (Pott's disease) is very prevalent — always consider when there is vertebral destruction and paraspinal abscess. MRI is mandatory — emergency if rapid progression.
recommendations.special_notes: EMERGENCY MRI when cauda equina syndrome is suspected (bilateral leg weakness, saddle anaesthesia, urinary retention/incontinence). Do not delay. Gadolinium essential if malignancy, infection, or inflammatory disease suspected.
contraindicated_if: pacemaker (non-MRI conditional) — use CT myelogram instead

recommendations.rationale: For bony detail — fractures, ossification of posterior longitudinal ligament (OPLL), spinal stenosis measurement, and surgical planning. Complements MRI.
recommendations.india_context: OPLL is an important cause of cervical myelopathy in Indian patients. CT myelogram used when MRI is contraindicated (pacemaker).
recommendations.special_notes: CT myelography: intrathecal contrast injection + CT — used when MRI contraindicated. Highly accurate but invasive.

recommendations.rationale: Gold standard when MRI is contraindicated (cardiac pacemaker, MRI-incompatible implants). Intrathecal contrast delineates cord compression, nerve root sleeve defects, and block.
recommendations.india_context: Important alternative for patients with pacemakers. Requires lumbar puncture and intrathecal contrast.

recommendations.modality: MRI Brain with contrast (including posterior fossa sequences)
recommendations.rationale: Best modality for posterior fossa pathology. Identifies cerebellar/brainstem infarct, acoustic neuroma (CPA mass), demyelination, tumour. CT is blind to posterior fossa due to bone artefact.
recommendations.india_context: Central causes of vertigo (brainstem infarct, cerebellar haemorrhage) must be excluded early. Acoustic neuroma (Vestibular Schwannoma) presents with unilateral sensorineural hearing loss and vertigo.
recommendations.special_notes: Use thin slices through posterior fossa and IAC (internal auditory canal). FIESTA/CISS sequences for CPA angle. URGENT MRI if: gait ataxia, falls, central nystagmus (direction-changing), other neurological signs.

recommendations.rationale: Emergency use when MRI unavailable and acute cerebellar haemorrhage is suspected. Limited for posterior fossa — beam hardening artefact from petrous bone. Cannot diagnose small infarcts.
recommendations.india_context: Use only in emergency when haemorrhage must be excluded and MRI is unavailable.
recommendations.special_notes: CT is poor for posterior fossa. Acute cerebellar haemorrhage may be visible but small infarcts will be missed.

recommendations.rationale: Dedicated MRI protocol for CPA/IAC masses (acoustic neuroma, meningioma). T2 FIESTA/CISS shows cranial nerves and inner ear structures with exquisite detail.
recommendations.india_context: Use when unilateral sensorineural hearing loss, pulsatile tinnitus, or asymmetric inner ear symptoms are present. Available at tertiary neurology centres.
recommendations.special_notes: Acoustic neuroma: T2 hypointense mass in IAC, enhancing on gadolinium. FIESTA shows VII and VIII nerve bundles separately.
